When listing your degree on your resume, follow this general format:

[Degree abbreviation] in [Field of study]
[Name of institution, location]
[Graduation year, optional]

For example:

B.S. in Computer Science
Massachusetts Institute of Technology, Cambridge, MA
2015

Here are some common degree abbreviations:

* Associate of Arts (A.A.)
* Associate of Science (A.S.)
* Bachelor of Arts (B.A.)
* Bachelor of Science (B.S.)
* Master of Arts (M.A.)
* Master of Science (M.S.)
* Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.)

You can also include any honors or distinctions you received during your education, such as cum laude, magna cum laude, or summa cum laude.

When listing your education, it's generally best to put the most recent degree first and work backwards in time. If you have multiple degrees in the same field, you can list them in reverse chronological order with the highest degree first.

For example:

M.S. in Computer Science
Stanford University, Stanford, CA
2018

B.S. in Computer Science
University of California, Berkeley, CA
2015 (cum laude)

I hope this helps! Let me know if you have any other questions.

For formatting your degree on your resume, follow these guidelines to ensure a professional and clear presentation:

1. Degree type: Start with the degree type, such as Bachelor's, Master's, or Doctorate. Abbreviate the degree type (e.g., "Bachelor's" instead of "Bachelor of Arts"). Capitalize the first letter, and do not use periods between the letters (e.g., "Bachelor" not "Bachelor.").
2. Major: Write your major field of study immediately following the degree type, in lowercase and title case (e.g., "Bachelor of Arts in Psychology" or "Master of Science in Computer Science").
3. Institution: Write the name of the institution where you earned the degree. Capitalize the first and last words, as well as any subtitles or words containing three or more letters (e.g., "University of California, Berkeley" or "Massachusetts Institute of Technology").
4. Graduation date: Include the month and year you graduated, or just the year if you prefer. Use a consistent format for all your degrees.
5. GPA (optional): If you feel comfortable including your GPA, you can add it after the institution name, in parentheses.

Here are a few examples to illustrate the formatting:

* Bachelor of Arts in English, University of Michigan, May 2018
* Master of Science in Electrical Engineering (3.8 GPA), Stanford University, 2020
* Doctor of Philosophy in Biology, Harvard University, 2025 (expected)

Remember to choose a consistent format for all your degrees and ensure that the information is accurate and up-to-date. Good luck with your resume update!
